terms.definition: A blueprint that defines the attributes and methods shared by objects created from it.
A concrete value built from a class, holding its own attribute data while sharing the class's methods.
The special method run when an instance is created, used to set up its initial attributes. Often called the constructor.
The conventional name for the first parameter of an instance method, referring to the instance the method is called on.
Data stored on an individual object (set via self), independent of other instances.
Data defined in the class body and shared by all instances of the class.
A function defined inside a class that operates on its instances (or, with decorators, on the class itself).
Defining a class that derives from a parent class, reusing and extending its attributes and methods.
A built-in that delegates to the parent class, commonly used to call the parent's __init__ or override methods.
A decorator that exposes a method as a read-only computed attribute, accessed without parentheses.
A special method named with double underscores (e.g. __init__, __repr__, __eq__, __len__) that hooks into Python's built-in behavior.
Treating an object by the methods/attributes it provides rather than its class — "if it has run(), it can act as a tool".
